directions.name: Human idea 7 Register reuse: keep the outer 64x384 hot path, but serialise the inner live set into 2x192 micro-panels
directions.hypothesis: The current correct 64x384 PTX path is no longer barrier- or mio-limited: barrier is only 6.58%, mio throttle is 0.33%, DRAM is 17.39%, but tensor active is still only 38.58% and warps active only 16.59% with launch__occupancy_limit_registers = 1. That is a classic register-wall signature. The highest-upside next move is therefore not another feed tweak first; it is to keep the outer CTA count advantage of 64x384 while cutting the live accumulator and B-fragment set roughly in half by issuing two serial 192-column subpanels inside the same fixed peeled kernel. This preserves the proven macro shape while targeting the actual limiter that remains on the correct branch.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Register pressure and resulting latency-hiding loss on the stable 64x384 PTX hot path.
directions.risk: Moderate. This stays on the correct branch, but it touches the hot PTX control path and can easily give back the CTA-count win if the extra serialisation adds too much steady-state overhead.
directions.stop_condition: Keep this family only if correctness stays PASS and registers/active-warps move in the right direction. Reject it if runtime regresses materially without a clear occupancy improvement.

directions.name: Human idea 7 Register reuse: if 2x192 still keeps too much live state, split the 64x384 hot path into 3x128 micro-panels
directions.hypothesis: The same register-wall diagnosis supports an even narrower phased schedule. If 2x192 does not drop the live footprint enough, a 3x128 serial panel schedule is the next step along the same family: keep outer 384 columns for CTA-count reduction, but only keep one-third of the B/accumulator live set resident at a time. This is lower-risk for correctness than reviving the old half-panel branch because it stays inside the already-correct 64x384 peeled kernel.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Register pressure first, then possible serialisation overhead if the panel size gets too small.
directions.risk: Moderate-to-high. It is safer than the broken half-panel family, but more aggressive paneling may increase control overhead enough to erase the occupancy win.
directions.stop_condition: Use only if 2x192 is either neutral or clearly promising but still register-limited. Reject if runtime loss comes mainly from serialisation overhead.

directions.name: Human idea 5 Bank conflict: stay on the correct 64x384 path and do a warp-local B-consumer load-order retune with no extra shared or CTA barriers
directions.hypothesis: If the phased-live-set family stalls, the fallback that best respects the user's hard constraints is a warp-local B-consumer retune on the same correct 64x384 kernel. That means changing the tile issue order or warp-local B fragment load order inside ptx_wmma_accumulate_tile_set_384, not adding a new CTA repack tile and not touching stage depth. This is lower ceiling than the register-wall attack, but it is auditable and safe to continue across multiple rounds if it shows even a small tensor-active gain without correctness risk.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Operand-delivery efficiency and shared-bank behavior inside the warp-local B consumer path, not DRAM throughput.
directions.risk: Lower upside. The current base already has low barrier and mio stalls, so this may only move noise-level performance.
directions.stop_condition: Only continue if the change is correctness-safe and does not increase shared memory footprint or CTA-level synchronisation.

notes: Round 1/10 human-idea audit against the fresh correct baseline: Tiling is accepted historically, but not as a fresh pivot this round because the repo already proved 64x384 is the best correct macro width for this GPU/shape; Coalescing Access is accepted as baseline because cp.async staging is already 16-byte aligned and the run is not DRAM-bound; Data Reuse is accepted as baseline because the fixed peeled kernel already depends on shared reuse; Async Copy is accepted as baseline because the current pipeline is already cp.async-based; Bank Conflict is deferred to rank-3 because the more immediate limiter on the correct branch is register-driven occupancy rather than feed stalls; L2 Cache swizzle is deferred because lts and dram throughput are both modest; Register Reuse is accepted as the primary family because the stable base is still sitting at only 16.59 active warps with launch__occupancy_limit_registers = 1; Pg2s is accepted as baseline because double-buffer G2S is already present; Ps2r is accepted in spirit via serial micro-panels that reduce simultaneously live B/acc tiles; Stage is accepted as baseline because barrier and mio are already low enough that stage churn is not the first lever. Recommended direction is dir_01 because it keeps the proven 64x384 outer shape while directly attacking the remaining register wall on the correct branch.
